mnemonics.title: Heap Property Reminder
mnemonics.content: P.A.R.E.N.T.S. = Parents Are always lARgER or Equal iN The heap maX-heap Structure

mnemonics.title: Heapsort Steps
mnemonics.content: B.S.S.R. = Build the heap → Swap max with last → Sink down the root → Repeat until sorted

mnemonics.title: Randomized QuickSort
mnemonics.content: R.A.N.D. = Random pivot selection → Array partition → New pivot position → Divide and conquer Recursively

mnemonics.title: Heap as Priority Queue
mnemonics.content: M.A.X. = Maximum element Always at eXit (root) for quick retrieval

mnemonics.title: Randomized Algorithm Benefit
mnemonics.content: E.X.P.E.C.T. = Expected time beats Pessimistic worst-Case through random Techniques

acronyms.usage: Describes heapsort process: extract max from heap, partition remaining, repeat

acronyms.expansion: Maximum Element At Root - Heap property
acronyms.usage: Parent nodes must be greater than or equal to children
Quicksort using random pivot selection to avoid worst-case O(n²)

acronyms.expansion: Random pivots Avoid Worst-case by selecting Median pivot Deterministic danger
acronyms.usage: Randomization in algorithms prevents pathological inputs
Heapsort guaranteed time complexity; randomized quicksort expected time

visualTips.concept: Binary Heap as Complete Binary Tree
visualTips.visualization: Draw a binary tree where level is filled left-to-right; root at top, leaves at bottom forming a triangle/pyramid shape - largest values near top, smallest near bottom
Index i has children at 2i+1 (left) and 2i+2 (right); parent at floor((i-1)/2) - picture heap as array with implicit tree structure
Imagine heavy rock sinking to bottom of water - compare with children, swap with larger child if needed, continue until in correct position
Start from last non-leaf node (n/2 - 1) and bubble down - like organizing a pyramid from bottom up, each level gets sorted before moving up
Random dot scattered across array line; partition places dots left of pivot (smaller) and right (larger) - visualize random scatter preventing worst-case patterns

analogies.analogy: Like a hospital emergency room: patient with highest priority (max) is always seen first (extracted), then next highest takes their place, until all patients served in priority order

Imagine building blocks in pyramid form - largest blocks must be at bottom for stability, but in max-heap largest is at TOP (root) for quick access - like having emergency supplies at tent entrance, not buried inside
Sorting a deck of cards by always picking a random card as reference - you shuffle first to avoid worst-case order (already sorted) - randomization protects against maliciously arranged input
Heavy particles sink to bottom of water - sink operation pushes large elements down to their proper position in heap, just like gravity settles heavy items

Like organizing people by height for a group photo: start from back rows, compare adjacent pairs, tallest move forward, continue until tallest are at front (root) - the heap property is enforced bottom-up
Las Vegas = always correct but random time (slot machine - unpredictable but honest); Monte Carlo = fixed time but maybe incorrect (gambling with approximation)
